mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.8% in Oct 2012 showing post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in < 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate accelerating consumer demand and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 5-trading-day window as market participants rapidly reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL growth slowing) signals sustained monetary accommodation, reducing real discount rates and supporting equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon as investors rotate into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 231.75 in Oct 2012
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary easing with price stability
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows no statistically significant outperformance versus single-series signals (interaction coefficient p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and future earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate margin compression.
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 (insufficient negative predictive power).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors, amplifying the multiplier effect on corporate earnings and equity returns over 20 trading days through interaction of construction and consumption channels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 850K annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 872K and RSAFS at 411.6B in Sept-Oct 2012
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth acceleration signals strengthening labor demand and future wage growth, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate labor cost pressures, producing mixed equity returns that depend on profit margin resilience over a 60-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133.8M in Oct 2012 showing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: labor market recovery with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in < 56% of triggered instances, or the signal shows negative correlation with returns in periods of rising wage inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% annualized).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL accelerating) signals monetary policy lag and future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate rate hikes and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 231.75 in Oct 2012
regime_hint: monetary policy tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns occur in < 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows weaker predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC interaction < |0.02|).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration below 2% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown and earnings recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure in anticipation of profit margin compression.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: Negative 60d returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating insufficient predictive power.
